narrative.summary:
📍 Hellbender
68-22 Forest Ave, Queens, NY 11385, USA

blocks.title: The Space
blocks.body: Forest Avenue in Ridgewood still feels like a neighborhood where people actually live, and Hellbender sits on that line between local bar and destination restaurant. Inside, tables pack close enough that you'll catch pieces of other conversations—save the gossip for post-dinner. They bring your milk for coffee in a tiny ceramic cow pitcher, the kind of detail that shouldn't matter but does.

blocks.title: The Food
blocks.body: The menu leans into comfort—egg tostadas at brunch, shrimp tacos that people order multiple rounds of, crispy potatoes you'll want as insurance against small portions. The cooking is thoughtful without announcing itself, the kind of place where technique shows up in texture and seasoning rather than descriptions. Expect to spend around $15-20 per plate and maybe order one more thing than you planned.

blocks.title: The Vibe
blocks.body:
⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Cute local bar and restaurant in Ridgewood.  It’s smart to make reservations especially for brunch and dinner times.  The tables are pretty close together so keep secrets till later.

The food is comforting and thoughtfully done but felt portions were on the small side.  We ordered the egg tostada, shrimp tacos, and a side of potatoes to share.  I loved the adorable little cow milk pitcher. It just made me smile.

The servers were kind and attentive even though they were pretty slammed."

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "From the moment me and friend walked in we felt like “yeah we’re about to have a good meal and good time.” Our waitress Lillian made our entire 1st time experience simply amazing. She was so personable, warm and explained the menu/drinks 👌🏾. The fried Oxacan cheese - 10/10, the roasted mushroom tacos - 10/10, himachi dish - 10/10, 3 cheese empanada 10/10, lamb dish -  a little gamey for my palette, but still good 7.5/10 and the drinks -10/10. Even though it was crowded it was still comfortable - we will definitely be returning for their famous brunch and dinner again and again! Hellbender is top tier!"

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Came here for brunch on a Sunday, had a reservation. Sat at the bar for a drink before we went to our table. Drinks were really good we had a few each, service was great. We ordered a few different things, the bloody Maria mignonette with the oysters was tasty and a little spicy. The pork tacos were incredible and of course we got the masa pancakes which were delicious but so filling. The jello of the day was a Concord grape jello with peanut butter drizzle and a homemade whipped cream which was really really good. Would love to come back here. And the bathrooms are huge!"
